user_prompt: Add a new view to `lib\FlyGuiViews` called `SplashView`
It is optionally the first view that is shown.
It loads `splash.png` from the internal LittleFS. It is a full screen 320x240 image. It writes it to screen. Then add a placeholder with a short delay, then hand off to `MainScreenView`
agent_reply: Added `SplashView` in [lib/FlyGuiViews/SplashView.h](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/SplashView.h) and [lib/FlyGuiViews/SplashView.cpp](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/SplashView.cpp).

It uses new `FLYGUI_VIEW_SPLASH = 0`, streams `/splash.png` from LittleFS to the full `320x240` display, waits `800ms`, then hands off to `FLYGUI_VIEW_MAIN`. If the image or filesystem load fails, it clears to black before handing off.
I couldn’t run a PlatformIO build because neither `pio` nor `python -m platformio` is available in this shell.

user_prompt: `lib\FlyGui` has a feature where a PNG file is loaded into a buffer, and this buffer can be reused in multiple views and items.
I need to remove this feature, the system will now draw by decoding on demand.
I have marked the section in `lib\FlyGui\readme.md` as struck-out
Remove this function from the actual code
The actual implementation will now use `lib\SpriteDraw\SpriteDraw.cpp` and maybe `lib\SpriteDraw\IconLookup.cpp` will be involved. `LittleFS` and actual file function will not be used.
agent_reply: Implemented.
I removed the RAM-loaded/reused PNG buffer path from FlyGui:
- Removed `imageBuffer`, `imageBufferSize`, `setImageBuffer()`, `findSiblingImageBuffer()`, and `findLoadedImageBuffer()`.
- Removed the LittleFS/SdFat image loading helpers from [FlyGui.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGui/FlyGui.cpp).
- Deleted the now-obsolete [SpriteCache.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGui/SpriteCache.cpp) and [SpriteCache.h](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGui/SpriteCache.h).
The new draw path decodes on demand:
- `FlyGuiItem::redraw()` resolves `imagePath_` through `IconLookup::fromString()`.
- It gets a compiled sprite via `IconLookup::getSprite()`.
- It draws through `SpriteDraw::drawPng()`.
I also removed the remaining LittleFS PNG draw from [SplashView.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/SplashView.cpp), so splash now uses the compiled `sprit_splash` asset and `SpriteDraw`.
Validation:
- `git diff --check` clean for the touched files.
- `pio run -e thefly_bringuptest` passed.

agent_reply: Implemented the display cleanup.
What changed:

- Defined as `M5GFX& thefly_display = M5.Display;` in [main.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/main.cpp)
- Removed `M5GFX& display` parameters from:
- `FlyGuiView::redraw`
- `FlyGuiItem::redraw`
- `FlyGuiText` redraw/text helpers
- `SpriteDraw::drawPng`
- `SplashView` / `ErrorView`
- Updated direct display users to use `thefly_display` instead of threading a local `display` reference or calling `M5.Display` directly.
- Kept `FlyGui::display()` as a convenience getter that returns the global display, so tree-based GUI code can still ask the GUI for its screen if needed.
Verification:
- `pio run -e m5stack-core2` passes.

user_prompt: if `gui->showView(FLYGUI_VIEW_MAIN)` happens after `bt_host_list->loadFromMicroSd()`, why does it seem like it took time for the phone and laptop items to load?
agent_reply: Yes, your instinct is right, but there’s a second thing making it look delayed.

`MainScreenView` is constructed early as a static in [all_init.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/all_init.cpp:32), before `setup()` creates and loads `bt_host_list`. Its constructor calls `syncBluetoothHostButtonFades()`, and at that point `bt_host_list == nullptr`, so phone/laptop start faded.
But the bigger issue is this: [SplashView.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/SplashView.cpp:31) automatically calls:
```cpp
gui()->showView(FLYGUI_VIEW_MAIN);
after the splash delay. That happens inside `show_splash()`, which currently runs before:
```cpp
bt_host_list = new BtHostList();
bt_host_list->loadFromMicroSd();
So the main view is actually shown once before the Bluetooth JSON is loaded.
Later, when `setup()` reaches:
```cpp
gui->showView(FLYGUI_VIEW_MAIN)

`FlyGui::showView()` sees that main is already the current view and returns immediately without `onLoad()` or forced redraw. Then a later normal poll/redraw finally runs `MainScreenView::redraw()`, calls `syncBluetoothHostButtonFades()`, and the icons catch up.

So the real cause is not just early construction. It’s that `SplashView` hands off to main too early, and the later explicit `showView(MAIN)` becomes a no-op. Best fix later: remove the splash view’s automatic transition and let `setup()` be the only place that transitions to main after all boot data is loaded.

user_prompt: make it so that the FlyGuiView polling issues the touch to the SplashView, the SplashView itself does the handover to MainScreenView.
we need `xTaskCreateUniversal` to run even if no touch happens
if an incoming bluetooth connection happens, it will cause a transition to the recording view, and when that ends, it should go to a dialog and then go to the MainScreenView
also, when finished drawing the text, force a full top bar redraw
agent_reply: Fixed the lifecycle.
Now:
- `wait_for_splash_touch` is completely gone.
- `SplashView::handleTouch()` receives the normal `FlyGui` polling event and performs `gui()->showView(FLYGUI_VIEW_MAIN)` itself.
- `setup()` no longer forces MainScreenView when SplashView is active, so `xTaskCreateUniversal(...)` still runs even if nobody touches the splash.

- If the app transitions to RecordingView from Bluetooth, `setup()` won’t stomp that view; the existing recording-finished path already shows the recorded-file dialog and then returns to MainScreenView.
- `draw_splash_boot_info()` requests and immediately performs a full top bar redraw after writing the firmware/security text.
Verified with:
```powershell
pio run -e thefly_sec0
Build passed.
Fixed the lifecycle.
Now:
- `wait_for_splash_touch` is completely gone.
- `SplashView::handleTouch()` receives the normal `FlyGui` polling event and performs `gui()->showView(FLYGUI_VIEW_MAIN)` itself.
- `setup()` no longer forces MainScreenView when SplashView is active, so `xTaskCreateUniversal(...)` still runs even if nobody touches the splash.

- If the app transitions to RecordingView from Bluetooth, `setup()` won’t stomp that view; the existing recording-finished path already shows the recorded-file dialog and then returns to MainScreenView.
- `draw_splash_boot_info()` requests and immediately performs a full top bar redraw after writing the firmware/security text.

Build passed.

user_prompt: read the flow diagram in `developer-docs\ui-design.md` and compare it against the current implementation, there will be things not implemented, and things maybe implemented differently
agent_reply: I compared `developer-docs/ui-design.md` against the current FlyGui/main flow. The short version: the broad skeleton exists, but the implementation has collapsed several diagram nodes into one reusable `ScrollView` plus `ModalDialog`, and the Cloud/Wi-Fi-connected path is much less complete than the diagram implies.
**Matches**
- Boot to Splash to Main exists. Splash stays up until touch: [SplashView.cpp](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/SplashView.cpp:20>).
- Top bar is global in `FlyGui::drawTopBar`, with date/time, battery, and disk warning: [FlyGui.cpp](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGui/FlyGui.cpp:665>).
- Main screen has common actions: Bluetooth, info, memo, phone/laptop quick connect, Wi-Fi: [MainScreenView.cpp](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/MainScreenView.cpp:32>).
- Recording has the thick red tally border: [RecordingView.cpp](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/RecordingView/RecordingView.cpp:447>).
- Memo recording exists and can cycle memo type while recording: [RecordingView.cpp](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/RecordingView/RecordingView.cpp:289>).
- Bluetooth connect/pair/info/delete are effectively implemented through the Bluetooth `ScrollView` context and dialogs.
- Wi-Fi AP mode exists as `WifiApModeView`, with credentials and live AP stats.
- Cloud upload progress exists as `CloudUploadView`: [CloudUploadView.cpp](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/CloudUploadView.cpp:91>).
- Recording stop goes to a finished confirmation dialog, then Main: [RecordingView.cpp](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/RecordingView/RecordingView.cpp:257>).
**Implemented Differently**

- `Bluetooth`, `WiFi`, and `Cloud` are not separate concrete screen classes. They are mostly contexts inside one `ScrollView`; the enum still has older-style placeholders like `FLYGUI_VIEW_BLUETOOTH`, `FLYGUI_VIEW_WIFI`, `FLYGUI_VIEW_WEB_ACTION`, etc., but those views are not registered in `init_gui`: [all_init.cpp](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/all_init.cpp:110>).
- `BTConnect` and `BTPair` are both represented by `ConnWaitingView` modes, not separate view classes: [all_init.cpp](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/all_init.cpp:180>).
- `InfoScreen`, `BTInfo`, `WiFiInfo`, `BTErrorDialog`, `WifiErrorDialog`, `CloudErrorDialog`, and finished confirmations are all `ModalDialog` with different text/icons/return views.
- `BTUnpairConfirm` is implemented as ScrollView delete mode plus a modal confirmation, returning to the same Bluetooth scroll context.

- Cloud upload completion always reboots after dismissing the completion/failure dialog, matching the diagram’s success path but also doing it for failure completion: [main.cpp](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/main.cpp:877>).
**Not Implemented Or Incomplete**

- `WiFiConnect --> established --> Cloud` is not currently wired. `scanAndConnect()` starts scan/connect, but once connected there is no callback registered to populate/show the Cloud scroll context. `populateCloud()` exists, but I don’t see it called anywhere.
- Selecting an individual Wi-Fi station does nothing right now: `onclick_wifi_station` only logs: [main.cpp](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/main.cpp:638>).
- `Cloud` as a user-visible menu is not reachable from the current flow, despite `ScrollView::populateCloud()` existing.
- `CloudEnroll` and `CloudEnrollConfirmation` are not implemented as GUI flows.
- `PasswordReset` and `MemoryReset` are web/AP administration features, not on-device GUI screens. The AP view intentionally has no stop/reset button except physical reset/power.
- `WiFiAP --> password reset/memory reset --> Boot` is therefore not a FlyGui transition.
- `BTConnect --> cancel --> Main` is slightly different: cancel returns to the remembered previous view, often the Bluetooth scroll, not always Main.
- `BTErrorDialog --> Main` is often `BTErrorDialog --> Scroll`, because connection errors return to the Bluetooth list.
- “Change volume during actual calls” does not appear implemented in the recording UI. There is mic/speaker mode and speaker mute behavior, but not volume adjustment.
- “Mute or unmute mic” is only partially aligned; the UI toggles audio mode and speaker mute, not a clear mic mute/unmute control.
**Global Event Policy**

Incoming Bluetooth connection to Recording is substantially implemented through pending flags and `on_bluetooth_state_changed`/recording handling. It applies broadly, with special suppression during pairing confirmation and with Wi-Fi AP activation shutting Bluetooth down, which matches the “except when Wi-Fi radio owns the radio” intent.

- `WiFiAP --> password reset/memory reset --> Boot` is therefore not a FlyGui transition.
- `BTConnect --> cancel --> Main` is slightly different: cancel returns to the remembered previous view, often the Bluetooth scroll, not always Main.
- `BTErrorDialog --> Main` is often `BTErrorDialog --> Scroll`, because connection errors return to the Bluetooth list.
- “Change volume during actual calls” does not appear implemented in the recording UI. There is mic/speaker mode and speaker mute behavior, but not volume adjustment.
- “Mute or unmute mic” is only partially aligned; the UI toggles audio mode and speaker mute, not a clear mic mute/unmute control.

Incoming Bluetooth connection to Recording is substantially implemented through pending flags and `on_bluetooth_state_changed`/recording handling. bool drawn = redraw(false);

The base `FlyGui`, `FlyGuiView`, and `FlyGuiItem` paths now aggregate whether display work happened, and the relevant overrides in FlyGui text/progress, views, PIN pad, and WAV playback now return `true` when they actually redraw and `false` when they skip.

commit_message: boot splash disk info and timezone

user_prompt: how many places in this code uses a parent view to capture a click event instead of letting the item handle it?
agent_reply: I’d count **5 parent-view touch handlers** that meaningfully capture a click/touch action instead of letting a normal `FlyGuiItem` own it:
1. [SplashView.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/SplashView.cpp:87)
Any press/release advances to main view. No item involved.
2. [QrCodeView.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/QrCodeView.cpp:29)
Any press/release dismisses the QR view. No item involved.
3. [ModalDialog.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/ModalDialog.cpp:65)
Any press/release dismisses the modal. No item involved.
4. [WifiApModeView.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/WifiApModeView.cpp:102)
Parent captures the eye area for reveal/hide and long-hold QR behavior. It uses `eyeItem_` for geometry/sprite, but does not call `eyeItem_.handleTouch()` or `trigger()`.
5. [ScrollView.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/ScrollView/ScrollView.cpp:463)
Parent captures left/right slot taps for scrolling. Center/exit/delete still go through items.

There is also a conditional parent capture in [FirmwareUpdateView.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/FirmwareUpdateView.cpp:108): once state is `Complete`, any touch restarts the device. In normal prompt state, it delegates to `goItem_` / `cancelItem_`.
So for haptics: putting click feedback only in `FlyGuiItem::trigger()` will miss those parent-captured actions. It will still cover the normal item button path.

user_prompt: correct mistakes I've made in my placement of `haptic_play_click();`, also, fix any missing included headers
agent_reply: Fixed the haptic placement/include issues.

I added missing `HapticsWrapper.h` includes to the GUI files that call `haptic_play_click()` directly, including [FlyGui.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGui/FlyGui.cpp:4), [FirmwareUpdateView.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/FirmwareUpdateView.cpp:7), [ModalDialog.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/ModalDialog.cpp:4), [SplashView.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/SplashView.cpp:4), [WifiApModeView.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/WifiApModeView.cpp:6), and [ScrollView.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/ScrollView/ScrollView.cpp:5).

I also removed the extra `haptic_play_click()` from `ScrollView::onDeleteButtonTriggered()`, because that path already comes through `deleteItem_.trigger()`, and `FlyGuiItem::trigger()` now clicks when a callback exists. Leaving it there would double-click.
Verified with `pio run -e thefly_sec1`, and it builds successfully.
Fixed the haptic placement/include issues.

I added missing `HapticsWrapper.h` includes to the GUI files that call `haptic_play_click()` directly, including [FlyGui.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGui/FlyGui.cpp:4), [FirmwareUpdateView.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/FirmwareUpdateView.cpp:7), [ModalDialog.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/ModalDialog.cpp:4), [SplashView.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/SplashView.cpp:4), [WifiApModeView.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/WifiApModeView.cpp:6), and [ScrollView.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/ScrollView/ScrollView.cpp:5).

I also removed the extra `haptic_play_click()` from `ScrollView::onDeleteButtonTriggered()`, because that path already comes through `deleteItem_.trigger()`, and `FlyGuiItem::trigger()` now clicks when a callback exists. Leaving it there would double-click.
Verified with `pio run -e thefly_sec1`, and it builds successfully.

agent_reply: Done. Diagnostics now tracks heap minima separately for:
`ih_min` = internal 8-bit heap minimum
`ps_min` = PSRAM 8-bit heap minimum
The line is shorter now, like:
```text
Changed [inc/diagnostics.h](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/inc/diagnostics.h:8) and [src/diagnostics.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/diagnostics.cpp:101).
Verified with `pio run -e thefly_sec1`: build passes. It still emits the existing `SplashView.cpp` `fontHeight` deprecation warning, but nothing from this change.

agent_reply: Done. I moved the shared PSRAM-first allocator into [utilfuncs.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/UtilFuncs/utilfuncs.cpp:239) and declared it in [utilfuncs.h](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/UtilFuncs/utilfuncs.h:49).

- [MicGainManager.h](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/MicGainManager.h:27): `setHighPassFilterEnabled()` / `highPassFilterEnabled()`
- [MicGainManager.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/MicGainManager.cpp:194): stores the boolean and resets HPF state when it changes
- [MicGainManager.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/MicGainManager.cpp:367): `high_pass_filter()` now returns the raw sample when disabled

- [AudioManager.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/AudioManager.cpp:1027)
- [AudioManager.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/AudioManager.cpp:1049)

user_prompt: hot-plug and hot-unplug isn't working inside `lib\WavPlayback\PlaybackView.cpp`, please fix, as similarly to how `lib\FlyGuiViews\RecordingView\RecordingView.cpp` as possible
agent_reply: Implemented the playback hot-plug/hot-unplug sync.

Changed [PlaybackView.cpp](<D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/WavPlayback/PlaybackView.cpp:541>) and [PlaybackView.h](<D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/WavPlayback/PlaybackView.h:49>) to mirror the recording view pattern: playback now tracks `ExtCodec::stateGeneration()` and calls `AudioManager::syncExternalCodecRouting()` on load and during redraw. That lets the existing AudioManager logic switch between SGTL5000 earbuds and NS4168 while playback is open.
If routing sync fails, playback now logs it and shows `Speaker routing failed` in the existing status area.
Verified with `pio run -e thefly_sec1`: success. There’s still the existing `SplashView.cpp` deprecated `fontHeight()` warning, unrelated to this change.

user_prompt: this is important, the left channel is floating, so in `EXTCODEC_NO_EARBUD` state, we need to only use right channel data, no mixing, no running AGC, there should only ever be one AGC ever
agent_reply: Fixed in [AudioManager.cpp](<D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/AudioManager.cpp:500>).
For `MicInput::LineInRight`, which includes `EXTCODEC_NO_EARBUD`, the external codec mic path now:
1. Reads SGTL5000 I2S stereo as before.
2. Copies only the right channel into `g_mono_buffer`.
3. Runs the single `MicGainManager::process()` once on that mono right-channel buffer.
4. Queues mono data directly to BT/file FIFOs.
So the floating left channel is no longer mixed, queued, or allowed to influence AGC/peak/silence-gate behavior.
I left the non-line-in-right path unchanged. Verified with `pio run -e thefly_sec1`: success. The existing unrelated `SplashView.cpp` deprecated `fontHeight()` warning remains.

user_prompt: where's the line of code where the security level is shown on the splash screen?
agent_reply: The splash screen shows it in [lib/FlyGuiViews/SplashView.cpp](/D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/SplashView.cpp:183):
```cpp
snprintf(text,
sizeof(text),
"FW: %s\nSecurity: %d",
BUILD_VERSION,
BUILD_WITH_SECURITY_LEVEL);
That’s inside `draw_splash_boot_info()`. The `Security: %d` text is the label/value, and `BUILD_WITH_SECURITY_LEVEL` is the compile-time security level being shown.

user_prompt: make that two snprintf calls instead of one, I might selectively disable the second one later
agent_reply: Done in [lib/FlyGuiViews/SplashView.cpp](/D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/SplashView.cpp:182).
It now builds the splash text with two `snprintf` calls: first the firmware line, then the security line appended separately so you can wrap or remove that second call later.

commit_message: removed security level display for 0 level

user_prompt: I have added a preprocessor define `ENFORCE_EXTERNAL_CODEC` to `conf.h`
in `main.cpp` `void setup()`, if `ENFORCE_EXTERNAL_CODEC` is defined, then failure to initialize the `ExtCodec` causes a fatal error with a screen, actually fatal and frozen.
agent_reply: Implemented in [main.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/main.cpp:130).
`setup()` now captures `ExtCodec::init()`, and when `ENFORCE_EXTERNAL_CODEC` is defined, a failed init calls:
```cpp
show_boot_error_f(true, "external codec init failed");
That uses the existing fatal boot error screen and stays frozen in the fatal loop.
Verified with `pio run -e thefly_sec1`, which passed. Only warning was the pre-existing deprecated `fontHeight()` warning in `SplashView.cpp`.

`setup()` now captures `ExtCodec::init()`, and when `ENFORCE_EXTERNAL_CODEC` is defined, a failed init calls:

That uses the existing fatal boot error screen and stays frozen in the fatal loop.

Done. I added `topBarLastPowerKeyIndicator_` in [FlyGui.h](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGui/FlyGui.h:127), and [drawTopBar()](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGui/FlyGui.cpp:361) now checks whether `BattTracker::powerKeyIndicatorActive()` changed since the last top-bar draw.
So the redraw gate now treats the ZZZ state as a reason to redraw immediately:
```cpp
const bool powerKeyIndicator = BattTracker::powerKeyIndicatorActive();
const bool indicatorChanged = powerKeyIndicator != topBarLastPowerKeyIndicator_;
const bool fullRedraw = forced || topBarNeedsFullRedraw_ || indicatorChanged;
That should stop it from waiting for the seconds value to change when the marker appears or disappears.
Verified with `pio run -e thefly_sec0`: build succeeded. There is one unrelated existing warning in `SplashView.cpp` about deprecated `fontHeight()`.
